title: Urban heat island mapping for climate adaptation
summary: Land surface temperature and land cover data identify urban heat hotspots for targeted intervention.
problem: Urban heat islands worsen health risks and energy demand during heatwaves, but city authorities often lack a spatial evidence base to identify the hottest areas and prioritise cooling interventions such as greening or reflective surfaces.
solution: CLMS land-surface-temperature and imperviousness products, combined with C3S climate data, produce composite urban heat-exposure maps. Authorities use them to target tree planting, cool-surface pilots and vulnerable-population support where heat stress is greatest.

copernicusRole.detail: CLMS land-surface-temperature and imperviousness products are combined with C3S data into a composite heat-exposure index. Copernicus supplies the layers; analysis builds the indicator.
